other_observers: Nathan Pieplow, Maggie Boswell, Bill Schmoker, John Vanderpool, Cole Wild, Andrew Spencer, Nick Komar; Nathan Pieplow and I are the original finders and identifiers of this bird.
specific_location: Sawhill Ponds Open Space, ponds # 13 and 5
description: A smallish Phoebe like Flycatcher with thin dark bill, dark back with a slightly darker tail and two faint wing-bars. The bird had a vaguely defined eye-line, a clean white throat, and a white breast with gray streaking. The bird's flanks and belly were salmon pink with the pink on the flanks reaching further towards the breast compared to the pink near the dorsal mid-line. The contrast between the streaked breast and the pink flanks and belly was distinct and not diffuse.Behaviors: The bird was sallying out from exposed perches flycatching around the edges of Sawhill Ponds 13 and 5. The bird infrequently wagged it's tail. In addition, the bird was aware of our presence and was fairly skittish although it approached to nearly 25 ft. For the first 30 minutes of observation, the bird was clearly patrolling a "territory" utilizing the same perches along the west side of Sawhill Pond 13. Before other observers arrived, the bird slowly made it's way across the northern edge of pond 13 and over to pond 5.Call: NonePlumage: adult
